items.definition1_source_equilibrium: For any source-equilibrium data $E$, $E$ is a source equilibrium iff: every chosen access action is feasible under $E.requirement$; for every student information state and every feasible action $a$, $u(info,a) \le u(info,a^*(info))$ for the chosen action $a^*$; and $E.estimationConsistent$ holds.
items.definition2_latent_skill_fair: A policy surface $S$ is latent-skill fair iff for every equilibrium $e$, skill $q$, and base group $b$, $S.latentAccessEstimate(e,q,b)=S.latentNoAccessEstimate(e,q,b)$.
items.definition3_observable_fair: A policy surface $S$ is observably fair iff for every equilibrium $e$ and base group $b$, $S.observableAccessEstimate(e,b)=S.observableNoAccessEstimate(e,b)$.
items.definition4_demographic_fair: A policy surface $S$ is demographically fair iff for every equilibrium $e$, $S.demographicAccessEstimate(e)=S.demographicNoAccessEstimate(e)$.
items.definition5_test_blank: A policy surface $S$ is test-blank iff for every equilibrium $e$, base group $b$, and test value $t$, $S.baseOnlyEstimate(e,b)=S.fullFeatureEstimate(e,b,t)$.
items.theorem3_1_optional_reporting: Assume each access fraction satisfies $0 \le \alpha_e(b)<1$. Then the school information set from access action with observed access set to false always has access status none. Moreover, for every equilibrium $e$ there is a strategy $W$ such that everyone takes the test, some base-score pair is not reported, reporting in each base group is by a score cutoff, and the base-mixed Gaussian posterior-law surface for $e$ is not latent-skill fair, not observably fair, and not demographically fair.
items.theorem3_1_report_required: Assume slopes are positive and each access fraction satisfies $0 \le \alpha_e(b)<1$. Then the school information set from access action with observed access set to false always has access status none. Moreover, for every equilibrium $e$ there is a strategy $W$ such that some base-skill pair does not take the test, taking in each base group is by a skill cutoff, and the base-mixed affine-skill posterior-law surface for $e$ is not latent-skill fair, not observably fair, and not demographically fair.
items.theorem3_2_optional_reporting_fairness_i: In the optional-reporting model, assume every $e$ is a source equilibrium with posterior-mean payoff, reporting is exactly the threshold rule $report(e,b,a)=true \iff d_e(b) \le a$, and if no positive-mass student in base $b$ reports then $baseOnlyEstimate(e,b)=fullFeatureEstimate(e,b,t)$ for all tests $t$. For the event-share binary-mixture estimate surface $S$, the observed-access information status is always none, and latent-skill fairness or observable fairness of $S$ implies test-blankness of $S$.
items.theorem3_2_optional_reporting_no_test_re: Under the same optional-reporting equilibrium, threshold-reporting, and no-reporter hypotheses, if the event-share binary-mixture surface $S$ is latent-skill fair or observably fair, then the observed-access information status is always none and there is no triple $(e,b,t)$ with $baseOnlyEstimate(e,b) \ne fullFeatureEstimate(e,b,t)$.
items.theorem3_2_report_required_fairness_impo: In the report-required model, assume every $e$ is a source equilibrium with payoff $1/2-\operatorname{UTM}(actorLaw_e(b),d_e(b))+actor$, taking is exactly the threshold rule $take(e,a,b)=true \iff d_e(b) \le a$, and if no positive-mass student in base $b$ takes then $baseOnlyEstimate(e,b)=fullFeatureEstimate(e,b,t)$ for all tests $t$. For the event-share binary-mixture estimate surface $S$, the observed-access information status is always none, and latent-skill fairness or observable fairness of $S$ implies test-blankness of $S$.
items.theorem3_2_report_required_no_test_relev: Under the same report-required equilibrium, threshold-taking, and no-taker hypotheses, if the event-share binary-mixture surface $S$ is latent-skill fair or observably fair, then the observed-access information status is always none and there is no triple $(e,b,t)$ with $baseOnlyEstimate(e,b) \ne fullFeatureEstimate(e,b,t)$.
items.lemma4_1_observed_access_strategy_proofn: If the fully specified optional-reporting data and fully specified report-required data are both source equilibria, with $testScale>0$, then in both models the chosen access action is $takeAndReport$ for every student information state.
items.proposition4_2_base_indexed_posterior_su: If the fully specified optional-reporting and report-required data are source equilibria, with $testScale>0$, then for any base-indexed one-test posterior-law surface with positive slope and positive law test scale, both models choose $takeAndReport$ for every information state, and that surface is not latent-skill fair.
items.proposition4_3_base_mixed_extra_signal_s: If the fully specified optional-reporting and report-required data are source equilibria, with $testScale>0$, then for any base-mixed extra-signal posterior-law surface with positive extra-noise variance, both models choose $takeAndReport$ for every information state, and that surface is not observably fair and not demographically fair.
items.definition6_resampling_policy: For any resampling experiment $e$, the resampling policy kernel is exactly $e.signalGivenBase$.
items.theorem4_4_resampling_policy: If the fully specified optional-reporting and report-required data are source equilibria, with $testScale>0$, then for every resampling experiment $e$, both models choose $takeAndReport$ for every information state, and the access-estimate kernel and resampling-estimate kernel are observably fair and demographically fair with respect to $e.baseProfile$.
